## We Say

When it comes to maintaining the elegance and longevity of leather surfaces in our vehicles or homes, we can't overlook the importance of using a high-quality leather protection product. Enter Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat, a groundbreaking solution that has caught our attention—and for good reason.

At its core, Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is engineered to provide an unparalleled level of protection for leather materials. This product utilises advanced nanotechnology, which forms a durable, hydrophobic layer on the leather surface. This means that not only does it repel water, but it also helps prevent dirt and grime from settling in—keeping your leather looking pristine for longer.

One of the standout features of Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is its ability to maintain the natural feel and appearance of leather. Unlike some other treatments that can leave a greasy residue, Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is designed to be non-greasy and transparent, ensuring that your leather retains its original texture and character. This is a significant benefit for those who appreciate the aesthetic qualities of leather and don’t want to compromise on its luxurious feel.

The magic of Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat lies in its formulation. Using a combination of specialised resins and polymers, this product creates a protective barrier that bonds with the leather. This bond not only offers protection against everyday wear and tear but also enhances the leather's resistance to UV rays, which can cause fading over time. By applying Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat, you are essentially extending the life of your leather items, making it a smart investment.

Additionally, the hydrophobic properties of this leather coat mean that spills and stains can be easily wiped away without the worry of permanent damage. Whether it’s coffee, soda, or a stray pet paw, Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat helps you stay one step ahead of potential mishaps.

We understand that the prospect of applying a new product can sometimes feel daunting. However, using Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is straightforward. Simply clean the leather surface thoroughly, apply the coating evenly, and allow it to cure. The result? A beautifully protected leather item that looks as good as new.

In our opinion, Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is a must-have for anyone serious about leather care. Its scientifically-backed formulation not only protects but enhances the beauty of leather, making it a standout choice in the market. If you’re looking to keep your leather looking luxurious and well-maintained, we wholeheartedly recommend giving Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat a try. Trust us; your leather will thank you!

## Description

Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat is a functional silica-based coating that forms a protective barrier layer on modern leather upholstery. It uses low-volatile silicon (Si) technology to lay down a film of dimethylsiloxane polymers that condense rapidly to form a flexible SiO2 coating with a lifespan of up to three months. Following application, treated surfaces actively repel liquids and other contaminants (including sweat and dye transfer), making them easier to clean and maintain. In addition, Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat also blocks out harmful UV radiation from the sun, thus helping to prevent the leather from fading over time.

## How To Use

Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat should be applied in a warm dry place out of direct sunlight. To protect your skin, we recommend wearing PB Nitrile Gloves during the application process.

In all cases, firstly clean the surfaces you are going to treat with either [Gyeon Q²M LeatherCleaner Natural](https://www.polishedbliss.co.uk/products/gyeon-q-m-leathercleaner-natural) or [Gyeon Q²M LeatherCleaner Strong](https://www.polishedbliss.co.uk/products/gyeon-q2m-leathercleaner-strong) and allow them to dry fully before continuing. To apply Gyeon Q² LeatherCoat, lightly wet a Meguiar's Even Coat Microfiber Applicator Pad with product and wipe it in evenly, using light pressure and overlapping strokes (aim to finish up with a thin, even layer). Once all of the surfaces are treated, leave them to dry naturally for a minimum of two hours before sitting in the car.
